Another major milestone for India's defence capabilities.

DRDO and the Indian Air Force have successfully tested the RudraM-II Air-to-Surface Missile, demonstrating precision, reliability, and long-range strike capability at nearly 300 km under demanding test conditions.

A strong step forward for indigenous defence technology and a testament to the skill of India's scientists, engineers, and air warriors.

tweets.title:
AMCA Engine: Rolls-Royce Makes Fresh Offer to India

British aerospace giant Rolls-Royce has submitted a new proposal to power India’s Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA), the country’s indigenous fifth-generation fighter program.

According to reports, Rolls-Royce is offering a co-development model with extensive technology transfer and local manufacturing, positioning itself against other contenders such as Safran and GE Aerospace in the race to develop India’s future fighter engine.
tweets.content:
AMCA Engine: Rolls-Royce Makes Fresh Offer to India

British aerospace giant Rolls-Royce has submitted a new proposal to power India’s Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA), the country’s indigenous fifth-generation fighter program.

According to reports, Rolls-Royce is offering a co-development model with extensive technology transfer and local manufacturing, positioning itself against other contenders such as Safran and GE Aerospace in the race to develop India’s future fighter engine.

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: 11/13
Nuclear Attack Submarines (SSNs): The Future of Undersea Warfare 

Unlike SSBNs, which carry strategic nuclear missiles, SSNs are hunter-killers designed to: 
• Track enemy submarines 
• Escort carrier strike groups 
• Gather intelligence 
• Project power far from home waters

Why SSNs Matters ?

SSNs combine unlimited endurance, high speed, and exceptional stealth, allowing them to operate undetected across vast oceans. Their versatility makes them indispensable for surveillance, strike missions, fleet protection, and maintaining blue-water naval dominance.

India's SSN Ambition

India's Arihant-class SSBNs provide strategic deterrence, but future indigenous SSNs will strengthen the Indian Navy's ability to counter adversary submarines, protect carrier battle groups, and maintain a persistent presence across the Indian Ocean Region and beyond.
tweets.content:
11/13
Nuclear Attack Submarines (SSNs): The Future of Undersea Warfare

Unlike SSBNs, which carry strategic nuclear missiles, SSNs are hunter-killers designed to:
• Track enemy submarines
• Escort carrier strike groups
• Gather intelligence
• Project power far from home waters

Why SSNs Matters ?

SSNs combine unlimited endurance, high speed, and exceptional stealth, allowing them to operate undetected across vast oceans. Their versatility makes them indispensable for surveillance, strike missions, fleet protection, and maintaining blue-water naval dominance.

India's SSN Ambition

India's Arihant-class SSBNs provide strategic deterrence, but future indigenous SSNs will strengthen the Indian Navy's ability to counter adversary submarines, protect carrier battle groups, and maintain a persistent presence across the Indian Ocean Region and beyond.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: 10/13
India's Nuclear Triad: 
A credible nuclear deterrent rests on three pillars: 
1. Land-Based Missiles – Agni series ballistic missiles 
2. Air-Based Delivery – Nuclear-capable fighter aircraft 
3. Sea-Based Deterrent – Arihant-class SSBNs armed with SLBMs 

Even if one or two legs of the triad are neutralized, the third can retaliate; ensuring a credible second-strike capability and robust nuclear deterrence. With the induction of the Arihant class, India joined the select group of nations with a fully operational nuclear triad.

Land. Air. Sea. 
Three Dimensions. One Deterrent.
tweets.content:
10/13
India's Nuclear Triad:
A credible nuclear deterrent rests on three pillars:
1. Land-Based Missiles – Agni series ballistic missiles
2. Air-Based Delivery – Nuclear-capable fighter aircraft
3. Sea-Based Deterrent – Arihant-class SSBNs armed with SLBMs

Even if one or two legs of the triad are neutralized, the third can retaliate; ensuring a credible second-strike capability and robust nuclear deterrence. With the induction of the Arihant class, India joined the select group of nations with a fully operational nuclear triad.

Land. Air. Sea.
Three Dimensions. One Deterrent.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: 9/13
The Arihant-Class 

But the most strategic component of India's submarine force lies elsewhere. Enter the Arihant Class. Arihant-Class SSBN SSBN stands for: Ship Submersible Ballistic Nuclear.

The Arihant-class is India's first indigenous nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarine (SSBN), forming the sea-based leg of the nuclear triad. Equipped with K-15 and K-4 SLBMs and powered by an 83 MW reactor, it provides stealthy, long-endurance patrols and a credible second-strike nuclear deterrent. 

Active Units 
• INS Arihant (S2) 
• INS Arighaat (S3)

Key Features 
🔹 Nuclear-powered propulsion 
🔹 Ballistic missile capability 
🔹 Advanced stealth and sonar systems 
🔹 Indigenous design and construction 
🔹 Cornerstone of India's strategic deterrence 

"Silent Sentinels of India's Nuclear Triad."
tweets.content:
9/13
The Arihant-Class

But the most strategic component of India's submarine force lies elsewhere. Enter the Arihant Class. Arihant-Class SSBN SSBN stands for: Ship Submersible Ballistic Nuclear.

The Arihant-class is India's first indigenous nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarine (SSBN), forming the sea-based leg of the nuclear triad. Equipped with K-15 and K-4 SLBMs and powered by an 83 MW reactor, it provides stealthy, long-endurance patrols and a credible second-strike nuclear deterrent.

Active Units
• INS Arihant (S2)
• INS Arighaat (S3)

Key Features
🔹 Nuclear-powered propulsion
🔹 Ballistic missile capability
🔹 Advanced stealth and sonar systems
🔹 Indigenous design and construction
🔹 Cornerstone of India's strategic deterrence

"Silent Sentinels of India's Nuclear Triad."

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: 7/13

The Sindhughosh-Class 
The INS Sindhughosh class (also known as Kilo-class) are Russian-built, diesel-electric attack submarines. They are optimized for anti-submarine and anti-shipping operations in shallow littoral waters. Known for their quiet operations, they are armed with heavyweight torpedoes and Klub-S anti-ship cruise missiles. 

Active Submarines 
INS Sindhuraj (S57) 
INS Sindhuratna (S59) 
INS Sindhukesari (S60) 
INS Sindhukirti (S61) 
INS Sindhuvijay (S62)
INS Sindhurashtra (S65) 
INS Sindhushastra (S65) 

Many naval analysts referred to them as "Black Holes" because of the difficulty in detecting them underwater.
tweets.content:
7/13

Each is designed for stealth operations, anti-ship warfare, anti-submarine warfare, intelligence gathering, and special forces deployment.

tweets.title:
Rajouri Anti-Terror Operation Enters Day 11

Security forces have intensified the ongoing cordon and search operation in the dense forested areas of Rajouri, J&K, to track down hiding terrorists.

Operations are underway in extremely challenging terrain marked by thick forests, steep hills, and difficult access routes. Forces are maintaining a tight security grid, carrying out extensive searches, and closely monitoring movement across the area.

Additional security measures remain in place to prevent any escape attempts as efforts continue to locate and neutralise the terrorists.

tweets.title:
Shaurya Chakra awardee Amit Kumar Rana, a MARCOS commando of the Indian Navy, has passed away in a road accident.

In Op Danna, he neutralized a terrorist at extremely close range while providing cover to Lt Cdr Mahesh Kumar, who neutralized another terrorist. In Op Shok Baba, Rana sir volunteered to advance under heavy enemy fire and place an IED on a fortified terrorist hideout, leading to the elimination of three terrorists. 

Rest in peace, warrior. Your service and sacrifice will never be forgotten.

tweets.title:
Iran suspends talks with U.S. over Israel’s Lebanon strikes

Iran has suspended exchanging texts with the U.S. over a preliminary agreement aimed at ending the war, in response to Israel’s escalating military strikes in Lebanon. Hours after Iran’s move, U.S. President Donald Trump said he had spoken with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Hezbollah, and that both sides agreed to stop attacking each other. He also said “talks are continuing with Tehran”.
tweets.content:
Iran suspends talks with U.S. over Israel’s Lebanon strikes

Iran has suspended exchanging texts with the U.S. over a preliminary agreement aimed at ending the war, in response to Israel’s escalating military strikes in Lebanon. Hours after Iran’s move, U.S. President Donald Trump said he had spoken with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Hezbollah, and that both sides agreed to stop attacking each other. He also said “talks are continuing with Tehran”.

tweets.title:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
1st Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The IDF eliminated Jamal Abu Aoun, a Hamas platoon commander in the military wing who operated as a doctor at Yafa Hospital in Deir al-Balah.

● The IRGC Navy announced that 15 vessels, including 4 oil tankers, safely transited the Strait in the past 24 hours under their coordination.

● Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf warned that the “bill” for Israel’s attacks on Lebanon is imminent, accusing the US of failing to enforce the ceasefire amid the naval blockade.

● Iran launched a ballistic missile targeting Ali Al Salem Airbase early today. Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted it, with reports of limited damage.

● A cargo vessel was struck by an unknown projectile in the Arabian Gulf, approximately 40 nautical miles southeast of Umm Qasr, Iraq, causing a large explosion on the starboard side.

● Tehran announced it has halted backchannel negotiations and text exchanges with mediators amid the recent escalation.

END OF THE BRIEF

1st Jun 2026:

- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The IDF eliminated Jamal Abu Aoun, a Hamas platoon commander in the military wing who operated as a doctor at Yafa Hospital in Deir al-Balah.

● The IRGC Navy announced that 15 vessels, including 4 oil tankers, safely transited the Strait in the past 24 hours under their coordination.

● Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf warned that the “bill” for Israel’s attacks on Lebanon is imminent, accusing the US of failing to enforce the ceasefire amid the naval blockade.

● Iran launched a ballistic missile targeting Ali Al Salem Airbase early today. Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted it, with reports of limited damage.

● A cargo vessel was struck by an unknown projectile in the Arabian Gulf, approximately 40 nautical miles southeast of Umm Qasr, Iraq, causing a large explosion on the starboard side.

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
31st May 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Iran’s IRGC intercepted and destroyed a US MQ-1 drone after it entered Iranian territorial waters early Sunday.

● The IRGC Navy reported that 28 vessels, including oil tankers and container ships, were granted permits to transit the Strait in the past 24 hours.

● The IDF foiled a Hezbollah plot to destroy the Qaraoun Dam on the Litani River to flood the area and obstruct advances toward Beaufort Castle and Ali al-Taher Ridge.

● Iran’s Khatam al-Anbia Central Headquarters stated that the Armed Forces now exercise full control over the Strait of Hormuz and all vessels must follow Iranian regulations.

● Iranian Ambassador to Qatar Ali Saleh Abadi met with Russian Ambassador Dmitry Dugadin to discuss bilateral ties and regional issues.

● As of today, CENTCOM has redirected 118 commercial vessels and disabled 5 to ensure compliance with the naval blockade.

END OF THE BRIEF

tweets.title:
India has reportedly issued a Letter of Request to France for a massive ₹3.25 lakh crore deal for 114 Rafale fighter jets.

The big headline?

94 of the 114 jets are expected to be manufactured in India, giving a major boost to indigenous aerospace production, jobs, and defence self reliance.
tweets.content:

tweets.title:
India’s Oman trade pact comes into effect as New Delhi looks to reduce vulnerability from the ongoing Strait of Hormuz crisis.

Unlike most Gulf states, key Omani ports like Salalah & Duqm lie outside Hormuz, giving India an alternative energy and trade gateway during regional instability.

Officials view the pact as both an economic and strategic move for long-term energy security.

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: MORE DETAILS EMERGE IN PAHALGAM TERROR ATTACK INVESTIGATION

Investigators have uncovered significant digital evidence linking the perpetrators to meticulous pre-attack planning. The National Investigation Agency (NIA) found that the two mobile phones used by the terrorists had originally been shipped to Pakistan by Chinese smartphone manufacturer Xiaomi. Supply chain records provided to investigators reportedly showed that the devices were delivered to addresses in Karachi and Lahore.

Forensic examination of the recovered phones revealed that the geographical coordinates of Baisaran Meadows, Pahalgam, had been plotted, screenshotted, and stored within the AlpineQuest navigation application. Investigators determined that these screenshots were taken on 15 and 16 April 2025, indicating that reconnaissance and operational planning for the attack had begun at least a week before the massacre.

According to the investigation, detailed analysis of data recovered from the phone of a terrorist killed during Operation Mahadev confirmed that specific points in the Baisaran area had been marked and preserved on the devices. The findings provide crucial evidence of premeditation and offer further insight into the planning behind one of the deadliest terror attacks in recent years.
tweets.content:
MORE DETAILS EMERGE IN PAHALGAM TERROR ATTACK INVESTIGATION

Investigators have uncovered significant digital evidence linking the perpetrators to meticulous pre-attack planning. The National Investigation Agency (NIA) found that the two mobile phones used by the terrorists had originally been shipped to Pakistan by Chinese smartphone manufacturer Xiaomi. Supply chain records provided to investigators reportedly showed that the devices were delivered to addresses in Karachi and Lahore.

Forensic examination of the recovered phones revealed that the geographical coordinates of Baisaran Meadows, Pahalgam, had been plotted, screenshotted, and stored within the AlpineQuest navigation application. Investigators determined that these screenshots were taken on 15 and 16 April 2025, indicating that reconnaissance and operational planning for the attack had begun at least a week before the massacre.

According to the investigation, detailed analysis of data recovered from the phone of a terrorist killed during Operation Mahadev confirmed that specific points in the Baisaran area had been marked and preserved on the devices. The findings provide crucial evidence of premeditation and offer further insight into the planning behind one of the deadliest terror attacks in recent years.

The issue between Nepal and India is an old one. I am seeing an unnecessary hype around Nepal's PM statement. He said nothing extraordinary, and it is being actively discussed between the two sides.

The reference to the UK in the discussion of key areas of discussion is unnecessary and might attract harsh reaction from Raisina Hills. We understand you want a counterbalance for India in the negotiations; that's why China and the UK are being involved, but things can go south really fast if that happens.
